title: Fractional CFO/Controller (Small Business) Kalamazoo Area
description:
**Nienhuis Financial Group** is a rapidly growing accounting firm committed to delivering excellence in fractional and managerial accounting services. Our client base includes private companies with between $1\-75M in revenue, varies in industry, and includes Not\-For\-Profit and For Profit. With a dedicated team and a strong focus on communication, accuracy and timeliness, we strive to exceed client expectations. As we continue to expand, we are seeking a skilled **Fractional CFO/Controller focused on small business** to build and maintain relationships with clients, provide financials, controller services and ensure compliance with accounting principles. This position will manage a team of 3\-5 employees.

 **To be successful for this role, you will have:**  

* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Strong business acumen
* Extensive knowledge of general financial accounting and cost accounting
* Strong technical and inter\-personal leadership
* Ability to lead teams including coaching, delegation, and performance management
* Highly proficient with accounting software
 **Education and/or Experience**  

* BA in accounting, business administration, business management, or other related field
* 10\+ years accounting experience

* 2\+ years supervisory or project lead experience

 **Preferred Education and/or Experience**  

* MBA or master’s in accounting, or other related field
* 2\+ years client management experience
 **Helpful Software Experience**  

Microsoft Outlook Email, Calendar, Teams, Excel, Multifactor Authentication; VPN/Terminal Server, QuickBooks Online, LastPass, Zoom, Canopy, OneDrive, Google Suite, Chrome, Adobe DC, VoIP/3CX, SafeSend, BambooHR, TSheets, Documo, Divvy, Bill.com, Fathom, Gusto.

  

**Certificates, Licenses, Registrations:** CPA preferred  

**Supervisory Responsibilities**: May or may not supervise a small team

  

**Full time:** Salaried, Exempt

title: Controller
description:
Controller

Location: Houston preferred  
Reports to: Chief Operating Officer  
Department: Accounting / Finance / Operations

Compensation:

Base: $150,000\-$180,000

OTE: $225,000

Role Summary

Acasia is looking for a highly capable Controller to own the accounting and controllership function and serve as the internal subject matter expert for all accounting operations. This role will report directly to the COO and will be expected to run the day\-to\-day accounting and finance function with a high degree of independence, rigor, and judgment.

The structure is simple: the COO and (fractional) CFO set direction, priorities, and financial strategy; the Finance/HR Manager supports execution and administration; and the Controller is the functional expert who ensures that the accounting and finance operation provides accurate and timely financial information, and that the accounting for Acasia is controlled and scalable.

This person should be capable of handling the accounting and finance function nearly end\-to\-end, while building the processes, discipline, and reporting foundation needed for a high\-growth company operating at the intersection of AI and infrastructure. Acasia is building the infrastructure layer powering the next generation of AI development.

What You’ll Own

The Controller will own the accounting function and be responsible for the integrity of the company’s financial records, reporting, close process, controls, and compliance.

Core responsibilities include:

* Own the monthly, quarterly, and annual close process and ensure timely, accurate financial reporting.
* Maintain the general ledger and oversee all core accounting activities, including accruals, prepaid expenses, fixed assets, revenue recognition, intercompany activity, and cash.
* Lead preparation of monthly financial statements and supporting schedules.
* Develop the information and data metrics for use by Acasia’s management.
* Build, document, and improve accounting policies, close procedures, and internal controls.
* Serve as the internal accounting expert and primary owner of technical accounting judgment.
* Partner with the COO on budgeting, forecasting support, cash visibility, and financial decision support.
* Work closely with the Finance/HR Manager to ensure clean execution of AP, payroll coordination, reimbursements, vendor setup, and other transactional workflows.
* Provide treasury leadership and that all disbursements are proper and that liquidity reporting is developed.
* Manage bank reconciliations, balance sheet reconciliations, and review of key account activity.
* Oversee revenue and expense recognition to ensure that Acasia’s financials are prepared in accordance with GAAP and reflect the business accurately.
* Support tax, audit, insurance, compliance, and other external reporting requirements in partnership with outside firms and internal leadership.
* Help establish a scalable accounting and finance infrastructure, with transparent reporting packages, and operating rhythms appropriate for a growing company.
* Identify process gaps early and proactively solve them before they become issues.
* Produce a accounting and finance function that leadership can rely on without excessive oversight.

What Success Looks Like

In this role, success means:

* The books are closed accurately and on time.
* Financial statements are reliable, decision\-useful, and well supported.
* The company has clean reconciliations, strong documentation, and improving controls.
* Leadership has confidence in the numbers.
* The accounting and finance function becomes more scalable, less reactive, and less dependent on heroic effort.
* The Controller can largely run the function, escalating only the right issues at the right time.

Ideal Profile

We are looking for someone who is not just an accountant, but a true operator within the financial operations of Acasia with a voice that isn’t suppressed. This person should know how to build order, create trust in the numbers, and own the function with maturity.

The right candidate will likely have:

* 7\+ years of progressive accounting / finance experience, with meaningful controllership or significant assistant controllership responsibility.
* Strong grasp of U.S. GAAP and practical accounting operations.
* Experience owning a monthly close process and producing financial statements and related data.
* Experience in a high\-growth company, startup, or operationally complex environment.
* Ability to work both strategically and hands\-on.
* Strong systems orientation and comfort working inside modern finance tools and ERPs.
* Strong attention to detail paired with sound business judgment.
* Confidence to act as the accounting SME while collaborating closely with leadership.
* Ability to create a structured accounting framework with a focus on continual enhancement.

Preferred Qualifications

* CPA strongly preferred.
* Experience with Sage Intacct or similar ERP is strongly preferred.
* Experience working with external auditors, bankers, tax firms, and outsourced support partners.
* Experience in SaaS, infrastructure, cloud, marketplaces, or other complex revenue environments is a plus.
* Familiarity with multi\-entity environments, intercompany accounting, and scaling controls is a plus.
* Experience with treasury and cash management functions in a well\-controlled environment.

Working Style

This role is a fit for someone who:

* Enjoys owning a function rather than just completing tasks.
* Can distinguish between what needs executive input and what they should just handle.
* Is comfortable building out the accounting framework while operating.
* Brings a high bar for accuracy without becoming bureaucratic.
* Can partner well with a COO and a finance manager in a lean, high\-accountability environment.

Why This Role Matters

This is a key hire for Acasia. The Controller will help establish the financial backbone of the company and create the discipline, trust, and operating cadence required to scale. This is not a passive bookkeeping role. It is a leadership seat within the finance function for someone who can take real ownership.

Pay: $150,000\.00 \- $180,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Spring, TX 77380

title: Associate Director, Accounting & Finance (Remote Operations & Service Line Lead)
description:
BOOST LLC is a growing professional services firm providing comprehensive back\-office solutions — including accounting, finance, contracts, procurement, pricing, HR, recruiting services and cybersecurity compliance services — to government contractors across the country. We’re in an exciting growth phase and looking for motivated contracts professionals to join us as we scale.


**Position Summary**

The **Associate Director** serves as the operational leader across all Accounting \& Finance service lines, acting as the primary execution partner to the Director. This role is responsible for ensuring all services are properly structured, resourced, and delivered with consistency, quality, and timeliness.


This is a high\-impact leadership role at the center of BOOST's accounting and finance practice. You will own delivery across a growing portfolio of GovCon clients, build and enforce the operational infrastructure that keeps the practice running at scale, and serve as the technical backstop when engagements get complex.


While the role is primarily operational and project management–driven, the Associate Director must also operate at a Controller level, providing technical oversight and stepping into engagements when needed. Non\-CPA candidates with equivalent depth will be considered, but CPA candidates will be prioritized for the technical demands of this role.


This role is remote with limited domestic travel for team interactions, and operational needs.


**Core Focus Areas**


* Cross\-service line delivery oversight
* Project management \& execution excellence
* Team operations \& capacity management
* Process standardization \& scalability
* Controller\-level technical oversight


**Key Responsibilities**


**1\. Service Line Oversight**

Oversee execution across all service lines:


* + Managed Accounting
	+ Implementations (e.g., Unanet, Costpoint, process buildouts)
	+ Fractional CFO support
	+ Compliance (ICS, Provisional Rates, Audit Support)
* Ensure each service line has:
	+ Defined scope and deliverables
	+ Standard timelines and workflows
	+ Appropriate staffing and skill alignment
* Identify gaps in delivery models and implement improvements


**2\. Project Management \& Delivery Execution**

Own end\-to\-end delivery across the full client portfolio:


* + Define scope, timelines, deliverables, and dependencies
	+ Ensure all work is planned, tracked, and completed on time
* Build and enforce structured project management practices:
	+ Task tracking systems (Asana or equivalent)
	+ Standard delivery cadences
	+ Weekly status and accountability reviews
* Proactively identify and resolve risks:
	+ Capacity constraints
	+ Missed deadlines
	+ Cross\-functional dependencies

**3\. Team Operations \& Resource Management**


* Own capacity planning across all service lines
* Align resources based on:
	+ Complexity of work
	+ Client priorities
	+ Team skillsets
* Monitor utilization, efficiency, and team performance
* Resolve competing priorities across engagements
* Partner with Director on hiring, team structure, and scaling strategy


**4\. Process Design \& Standardization** Build and enforce standardized workflows across services:


* + Month\-end close and reporting
	+ Implementation project plans
	+ Compliance timelines (ICS, rates, audits)
* Establish consistent operating models across clients
* Maintain and evolve SOPs for all recurring processes
* Drive scalability through process optimization and automation where possible


**5\. Client Delivery Accountability**


* Own overall delivery quality and timeliness across all engagements
* Act as escalation point for delivery breakdowns or client concerns
* Ensure consistent client experience across all service lines
* Provide visibility to Director on:
	+ Delivery risks
	+ Team performance
	+ Client health
* Step into client\-facing situations as needed to stabilize or support


**6\. Controller\-Level Technical Oversight** Provide technical review and guidance across engagements:


* + Month\-end close and financial reporting
	+ Balance sheet integrity and reconciliations
	+ Revenue recognition and cost allocation
* Support complex and high\-risk areas:
	+ Incurred Cost Submissions (ICS)
	+ Provisional rate development
	+ DCAA/FAR compliance
* Step in as acting Controller when:
	+ Coverage gaps arise
	+ Engagement complexity increases
	+ Teams require additional support or oversight

**7\. Deputy to the Director**


* Act as second\-in\-command across Accounting \& Finance operations
* Translate strategic direction into execution across service lines
* Lead implementation of:
	+ New service offerings
	+ Process improvements
	+ Organizational structure changes
* Provide regular reporting on:
	+ Delivery health across service lines
	+ Team capacity and utilization
	+ Operational risks and improvement opportunities

**Qualifications**


**Required**


* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance or equivalent experience
* 10–12\+ years of progressive experience in accounting/finance
* Proven experience operating at a Controller level or equivalent
* Demonstrated success managing multi\-service line or multi\-client delivery environments
* Strong project management and operational leadership experience


**Preferred**


* Government contracting experience (FAR, DCAA, ICS, indirect rates)
* Experience in consulting or outsourced accounting environments
* CPA strongly preferred
* Experience with:
	+ Deltek Costpoint
	+ Unanet
	+ QuickBooks \& QBO
	+ Project management tools (Asana or similar)

**Core Competencies**


* **Operational Leadership** – Oversees and drives execution across service lines
* **Project Management Excellence** – Ensures structured, on\-time delivery
* **Controller\-Level Judgment** – Provides technical oversight and guidance
* **Scalability Mindset** – Builds processes that support growth
* **Resource Optimization** – Aligns people to priorities effectively
* **Accountability \& Ownership** – Drives results across teams


**Success in This Role Looks Like**


* All service lines operate with clear structure, timelines, and accountability
* Client deliverables are consistently on time and high quality
* Team capacity is optimized with minimal reactive fire drills
* Technical accounting risks are identified and resolved early
* Delivery is scalable, repeatable, and not dependent on individuals
* Director is fully focused on growth, strategy, and executive relationships


**Position Structure**


* Reports to: Director of Accounting \& Finance
* Direct Reports: Managers, Sr. Accountants, and technical support team members.
* Role Type: Operational leadership with technical oversight across the full practice


**All In Compensation**


* $190,000 \- $215,000
* **Base Salary:** $165,000 – $180,000
* **Performance Bonus:** Target 15%–25% based on individual and team performance


**Why BOOST**


* Opportunity to grow with a scaling GovCon consulting firm
* Direct input over how the Accounting \& Finance practice is built and operated
* Cross\-functional collaboration across BOOST’s full suite of back\-office professional Team that lives its core values, shows up for each other and drives real impact for the clients they serve


**BOOST LLC is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other legally protected status.**

title: Fractional CFO
description:
We are expanding our team and searching for a Fractional (Part\-time) Chief Financial Officers in Massachusetts.

**Who are we?**

The CFO Centre Group is the global no. 1 provider of portfolio CFO services and, to support growing demand, we are looking to add to our team of CFOs to deliver the company’s unique style of portfolio CFO services: delivering time, money and peace of mind to entrepreneurial owner managers of small to mid size businesses ($2M \- $100M).

SMBs typically do not need, or want, a full\-time CFO but do need ongoing financial leadership. Our model provides them with a flexible, affordable option to acquire this skill set from a business whose purpose is making a real difference to our worldwide client base.

**What’s the opportunity?**

We’re expanding our team in Massachusetts and are looking for highly experienced, client\-focused CFOs with entrepreneurial drive to work closely with the business owners/CEOs of SMBs in the New England area.

If you’re an experienced Chief Financial Officer and you're looking for variety and flexibility in your working life, the chance to build a business, as well as to be part of a local, national and global team, then read on, this might be for you.

The role is self\-employed, whilst operating within a defined model, combining the benefits of being part of a team and supported by a well\-established organisation, but with a level of autonomy and freedom in how you deliver outcomes for clients.

**What do we do?**

We define and deliver the numbers that *really* matter. What does that mean? Well, anyone can come up with numbers, the difference with us is that we help our clients find the numbers that *really* make a difference, not only to their business, but to their lives, and help deliver them! Whether that’s growth, acquisition, consolidation, exit or setting them up for the lifestyle they’ve always wanted.

*“Working with The CFO Centre has been a game changer for me and my business. Not only have we doubled our revenue but I’m now able to focus on the things that really matter in the business now”. – CEO, Client Company*

**Could this be for you?**

You’ll join a collective team of CFOs who, between them, bring together thousands of years of collective experience to supercharge our clients’ businesses. We are 750\+ people, in 5 continents, across 17 countries – a truly global team.

*“I started here 4 years ago and I’m loving the lifestyle and loving the difference I can make to the clients I’m working with.”*

**Do you have experience in any of the following?**

· Driving strategic direction of a business

· Increasing profit

· Sourcing funding

· Improving cash flow

· Selling/exiting businesses

· Tightening up compliance, Improving systems and controls

· Optimizing tax and legal

. Implementing KPI reporting

· Mergers and acquisitions

· Identifying risks, Reviewing business plans

**The ideal candidate will be:**

· A strong ‘people person’ with a natural ability to build relationships;

· A ‘team player’ who thrives on contributing to a high performance team;

· Highly networked and love meeting new people, developing business and ensuring client satisfaction;

· Energetic, motivated, able to make things happen; with a desire to build businesses;

· Organized and dependable;

· Entrepreneurial and non\-corporate, seeking the freedom to work semi\-autonomously;

· A life\-long learner.

**Your Qualifications:**

· You have worked as a CFO for a minimum of 5 years during your finance career;

· You have a professional accounting qualification or a finance degree and / or an MBA;

· You have a breadth of sector and transactional experience.

For more information, please visit www.cfocentre.com/us/join\-the\-team/

*At the CFO Centre, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where all who join us are valued, respected and given equal opportunities to thrive. We embrace diversity in all its forms, echoing our values of being Open, Progressive and Passionate and believe that it enriches and strengthens our organisation. We encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds and perspectives and we celebrate the unique contributions they bring so that together we can help change the lives of our clients.*

Pay: Up to $250,000\.00 per year

Application Question(s):

* Do you have a CPA or MBA?

Experience:

* CFO: 5 years (Required)

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Worcester, MA 01610
company_description: The CFO Centre provides part-time CFOs to entrepreneurs & managers of SME businesses who don’t want, don’t need or can’t afford a full-time CFO but benefit from the skill set to reach their goals.

title: Financial Controller
description:
Serving Indianapolis and the surrounding counties since 1905, the IndyHumane provides vital services to animals and people alike through sheltering and adopting animals, providing enrichmentfor adoptable animals through our enrichment programs, and outreach through our community and shelter programs.


The position requires strong collaboration and communication skills to support non\-financial team members in successfully driving the mission. The position is responsible for assuring all financial transactions are accurately and completely recorded, running a smooth month end close to produce financial reports within specific deadlines. Activities include monitoring grant performing reconciliations, assisting with tax compliance and audits, and providing financial analysis.


RESPONSIBILITIES \& DUTIES

* Ensure accurate and timely recording of all revenue by reconciling source systems to bank activity and preparing necessary journal entries.
* Partner cross\-functionally with departments to provide relevant financial insights, supporting informed decision\-making and operational effectiveness.
* Review and approve check requests and organizational expenditures to ensure alignment with the approved budget, internal controls, and compliance requirements.
* Maintain and update the organization’s Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) manual to reflect current processes and best practices.
* Collaborate with program teams to track and manage temporarily restricted funds, ensuring proper use, release, and compliance with donor restrictions.
* Reconcile and maintain balance sheet accounts, including complex cash activity across multiple systems, ensuring accuracy and completeness to support financial reporting, management analysis, and audit readiness.
* Support the Fractional CFO with financial analysis, budgeting, and strategic projects to inform organizational decision\-making.
* Apply strong analytical, creative, and problem\-solving skills to address operational and financial challenges within resource constraints.
* Assist in the preparation and filing of tax returns and other regulatory documents to ensure timely compliance.
* Provide training and financial guidance to staff across the organization, including non\-finance personnel.
* Supervise and develop a team of two staff members, ensuring performance goals are met and HR deadlines are completed.
* Perform additional duties as assigned to support organizational priorities


MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS

* BA/BS in Accounting or Finance is required. CPA is a plus.
* Five years of experience in accounting with at least two years in non\-profits.
* Must have strong computer skills and experience with Microsoft suite of products.
* Strong verbal and written communication skills
* Strong interpersonal skills with customer service orientation.
* Ability to utilize creative and critical thinking to develop innovative solutions and effectively address unique challenges using available resources.
* Ability to multi\-task, work under pressure and meet deadlines required.
* Experience with Sage Intact or another ERP systems a plus.

  

Physical setting:

* Office


Schedule:

* 8 hour shift
* Day shift
* Monday to Friday
* Weekends as needed


Ability to commute/relocate:

* Indianapolis, IN: Reliably commute or planning to relocate before starting work (Required)


Work Location: In person

title: Controller
description:
**Who We Are**



Richmond Vona, LLC is a personal injury law firm located in Buffalo, NY, that’s changing the game for both client and employee experience. We’re setting a new standard in how personal injury law is practiced and delivered.



Our mission is to provide a superior level of service to our clients through personal connection and continuous communication, while also creating a high\-potential, rewarding workplace for our exceptional team.

  

We’ve been recognized as one of Inc. 5000’s Best Workplaces in America and named one of the Fastest\-Growing Private Companies, ranking as the 4th fastest\-growing law firm in the country. Locally, we’ve also been honored as a Buffalo Business First Best Places to Work. These recognitions reflect our commitment to building something different for both our clients and our people.

  

We believe great results come from great people, strong systems, and a culture rooted in accountability, professionalism, and teamwork. Our core values, Winning, Always Ready, Precision, High\-Level Communication, Professionalism, Service, and Accountability, guide how we operate every day.

  

This is a rare opportunity to join a high\-performing organization during an exciting growth phase and help build the financial infrastructure that supports long\-term success.

 **The Opportunity**



Richmond Vona is seeking an experienced Controller to lead and elevate the firm’s accounting and financial operations. This role is ideal for a hands\-on accounting leader who thrives in a fast\-paced environment, enjoys building systems, solving problems, and thinking beyond the numbers.

  

The Controller will oversee all core accounting functions while serving as a strategic business partner to ownership and leadership. This person must be both detail\-oriented and forward\-thinking — someone who understands how day\-to\-day accounting impacts the bigger picture of profitability, growth, and operational excellence.

  

Reporting directly to executive leadership and working closely with our external CPA partners and Fractional CFO, the Controller will play a critical role in helping the firm scale intelligently and efficiently.

 **What Success Looks Like**



The right person for this role is:


* A self\-starter who takes ownership and moves things forward without waiting to be asked
* Comfortable operating in a high\-growth, fast\-moving environment
* Curious, coachable, and eager to continuously learn
* Able to balance tactical execution with strategic thinking
* A strong communicator who can translate financial data into business insight
* A leader who builds trust, accountability, and high standards
* Someone who values process improvement, efficiency, and excellence
* Energized by helping build something special
* Comfortable owning problems, identifying root causes, and implementing lasting solutions
  


**Key Responsibilities**


**Financial Leadership \& Accounting Operations**


* Lead all day\-to\-day accounting functions, including accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll, billing, general ledger, reconciliations, and month\-end close
* Ensure timely, accurate, and complete financial reporting on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis
* Maintain accounting records and financial systems with a high level of accuracy and organization
* Manage cash flow, banking relationships, and liquidity planning
* Oversee trust/client funds handling and ensure proper controls and compliance
* Oversee settlement disbursements, referral fee payments, and related financial controls
* Maintain and strengthen internal controls to reduce risk, improve accuracy, and support accountability
* Support compliance with law firm accounting requirements, including trust accounting and client fund management, as applicable
  


**Strategic Finance \& Decision Support**


* Prepare budgets, forecasts, and financial models to support business planning
* Analyze trends, KPIs, margins, and profitability by department or practice area
* Provide leadership with clear financial insights to support decision\-making
* Identify risks, opportunities, and cost\-saving initiatives
* Help leadership understand the financial impact of growth initiatives and investments
* Develop reporting and dashboards that improve visibility into financial performance and key business drivers
  


**Process Improvement \& Scalability**


* Build and improve systems, workflows, and internal controls
* Create more efficient processes that support future growth
* Evaluate technology and automation opportunities across accounting functions
* Develop documentation and SOPs to ensure consistency and accountability
  


**Leadership \& Collaboration**


* Partner closely with Operations, HR, Marketing, Ownership, and outside advisors
* Manage and mentor accounting or administrative team members as the department grows
* Foster a culture of accountability, professionalism, responsiveness, and continuous improvement
* Communicate clearly and proactively across departments
* Work closely with the firm’s external CPA partners and Fractional CFO to support accurate reporting, planning, and financial decision\-making
  


**Qualifications**


**Required**

* Strong understanding of GAAP, financial reporting, reconciliations, and internal controls
* Experience managing full\-cycle accounting operations
* Advanced proficiency in accounting systems such as QuickBooks Online or similar platforms
* Strong Excel and financial analysis skills
* Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail
  


**Strongly Preferred**


* CPA or CPA\-track
* Experience in **professional services industries** (law firm, consulting, accounting firm, medical practice, agency, etc.)
* Experience in a growing entrepreneurial company

**Why Join Richmond Vona?**


**Compensation**



$90,000 – $120,000 based on experience, qualifications, and leadership background. Performance\-based bonus opportunities available.

  

**Benefits**

* 401(k) with firm contribution
* Paid time off
* Hybrid flexibility after the onboarding period

* Ongoing professional development opportunities
  


**Career Growth**



We are growing quickly, and this role offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact while growing with the organization. For the right leader, this can evolve into a broader finance leadership role over time.

  

**Our Core Values in Action**

  


We are looking for someone who naturally aligns with our values:

**Ready to Apply?**

  


If you are an accounting leader who wants more than a traditional back\-office role and you’re excited to help build a high\-performing, people\-first organization, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Consultant CFO
description:
Job description:

We are expanding our team and searching for a Fractional (Part\-time) Chief Financial Officers in the **Greater Boston area.**

**Who are we?**

The CFO Centre Group is the global no. 1 provider of portfolio CFO services and, to support growing demand, we are looking to add to our team of CFOs to deliver the company’s unique style of portfolio CFO services: delivering time, money and peace of mind to entrepreneurial owner managers of small to mid size businesses ($2M \- $100M).

SMBs typically do not need, or want, a full\-time CFO but do need ongoing financial leadership. Our model provides them with a flexible, affordable option to acquire this skill set from a business whose purpose is making a real difference to our worldwide client base.

**What’s the opportunity?**

We’re expanding our team in Boston and are looking for highly experienced, client\-focused CFOs with entrepreneurial drive to work closely with the business owners/CEOs of SMBs in and around Boston.

If you’re an experienced Chief Financial Officer and you're looking for variety and flexibility in your working life, the chance to build a business, as well as to be part of a local, national and global team, then read on, this might be for you.

The role is self\-employed, whilst operating within a defined model, combining the benefits of being part of a team and supported by a well\-established organisation, but with a level of autonomy and freedom in how you deliver outcomes for clients.

**What do we do?**

We define and deliver the numbers that *really* matter. What does that mean? Well, anyone can come up with numbers, the difference with us is that we help our clients find the numbers that *really* make a difference, not only to their business, but to their lives, and help deliver them! Whether that’s growth, acquisition, consolidation, exit or setting them up for the lifestyle they’ve always wanted.

*“Working with The CFO Centre has been a game changer for me and my business. Not only have we doubled our revenue but I’m now able to focus on the things that really matter in the business now”. – CEO, Client Company*

**Could this be for you?**

You’ll join a collective team of CFOs who, between them, bring together thousands of years of collective experience to supercharge our clients’ businesses. We are 750\+ people, in 5 continents, across 17 countries – a truly global team.

*“I started here 4 years ago and I’m loving the lifestyle and loving the difference I can make to the clients I’m working with.”*

**Do you have experience in any of the following?**

· Driving strategic direction of a business

· Increasing profit

· Sourcing funding

· Improving cash flow

· Selling/exiting businesses

· Tightening up compliance, Improving systems and controls

· Optimizing tax and legal

. Implementing KPI reporting

· Mergers and acquisitions

· Identifying risks, Reviewing business plans

**The ideal candidate will be:**

· A strong ‘people person’ with a natural ability to build relationships;

· A ‘team player’ who thrives on contributing to a high performance team;

· Highly networked and love meeting new people, developing business and ensuring client satisfaction;

· Energetic, motivated, able to make things happen; with a desire to build businesses;

· Organized and dependable;

· Entrepreneurial and non\-corporate, seeking the freedom to work semi\-autonomously;

· A life\-long learner.

**Your Qualifications:**

· You have worked as a CFO for a minimum of 5 years during your finance career;

· You have a professional accounting qualification or a finance degree and / or an MBA;

· You have a breadth of sector and transactional experience.

For more information, please visit www.cfocentre.com/us/join\-the\-team/

*At the CFO Centre, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where all who join us are valued, respected and given equal opportunities to thrive. We embrace diversity in all its forms, echoing our values of being Open, Progressive and Passionate and believe that it enriches and strengthens our organisation. We encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds and perspectives and we celebrate the unique contributions they bring so that together we can help change the lives of our clients.*

Pay: Up to $250,000\.00 per year

Application Question(s):

* Do you have an MBA or CPA?

Experience:

* CFO: 5 years (Required)

Work Location: In person

title: Accounting (CAS) Manager - Public Accounting
description:
**About Us:**

Harvey, Tully \& Associates is a growing Accounting and Tax firm based in Plano, Texas, specializing in tax advisory, preparation, and planning, as well as comprehensive accounting services for small and medium\-sized businesses. From bookkeeping to fractional CFO support, we provide tailored solutions to help our clients thrive.

What sets us apart? At Harvey, Tully \& Associates, we offer exposure to the **entire accounting cycle and related taxation**, giving you the opportunity to grow your expertise across a broad range of services. Unlike larger firms that often confine employees to narrow tasks, we empower our team members to work on diverse projects, from financial reporting to strategic tax planning. This hands\-on experience, combined with a collaborative team of CPAs, EAs, and experienced accountants, ensures you’ll develop a well\-rounded skill set in a supportive environment.

**The Role:**

We are looking for an experienced and driven Accounting Manager to take the lead in managing the accounting needs of a diverse client portfolio. This role is ideal for a proactive professional who thrives on providing strategic financial insights and managing complex accounting needs.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Manage a portfolio of clients, including businesses with advanced accounting needs.
* Prepare and review financial statements, budgets, and cash flow analyses.
* Provide strategic financial advice and planning to help clients achieve business goals.
* Perform monthly and quarterly accounting activities, including reconciliations and reporting.
* Collaborate with the tax advisory team to integrate accounting and tax planning services.
* Build and maintain strong client relationships through proactive communication and exceptional service.
* Lead, mentor, and develop accounting staff, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and professional growth.

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
* CPA designation is a plus but not required.
* Public accounting experience is a requirement.
* 5\+ years of relevant accounting experience, preferably with small to medium\-sized businesses.
* Strong understanding of accounting principles, financial reporting, and budgeting.
* Experience in client\-facing roles with a consultative approach is a plus.
* Proficiency in QuickBooks Online is a requirement.
* Excellent communication and organizational skills.
* A proactive, problem\-solving mindset.

**What We Offer:**

* Competitive salary based on experience.
* A supportive and collaborative team environment.
* Healthcare, vision, 401k and dental
* Two weeks of holiday off at the end of the year.
* Federal holidays.
* Early closure at 4 PM every Friday.
* Opportunities for professional development and career advancement, potential for performance bonus.

**Location:**  
This position is based in Plano, TX.

**How to Apply:**

If you’re an experienced accountant looking for a role where you can make an impact, gain exposure to diverse financial processes, and grow your career, we’d love to hear from you! Please send your resume and cover letter to info@htaadvisors.com.

Join Harvey, Tully \& Associates and take the next step in your accounting career!

Job Type: Full\-time

Benefits:

* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Vision insurance

Application Question(s):

* What is your desired salary?

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Plano, TX 75075

title: Fractional CFO
description:
At Lucida, we're more than just a full\-service accounting firm; we're a community of over 100 professionals across four locations, dedicated to providing exceptional service. As a proud member of the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants, Wisconsin Institute of Certified Public Accountants, and the National Association of Certified Valuation Analysts, we specialize in supporting closely held companies in diverse sectors including healthcare, real estate, construction, manufacturing, the arts, and more.

Our mission is to simplify complexities for our clients, offering a collaborative environment that fosters innovative strategies and operational excellence.  



As a **Fractional CFO** you will be embedded within our CAS practice, serving as a strategic financial advisor to a portfolio of the firm’s clients. This is a client\-facing role—you will act as an extension of our firm, **delivering CFO\-level insight and guidance to business owners and leadership teams who do not have (or do not need) a full\-time CFO.** You will work closely with our internal CAS, tax, and payroll teams to ensure clients receive seamless, integrated financial leadership experience.  


**Duties/Responsibilities:**

Financial Strategy \& Planning
* Serve as the outsourced CFO for an assigned portfolio of CAS clients, acting as their primary strategic financial advisor
* Develop and maintain client budgets, cash flow forecasts, and long\-range financial projections
* Build and refine financial models to support client decision\-making around growth, hiring, capital expenditures, and pricing
* Establish and monitor KPIs and financial dashboards tailored to each client’s industry and goals
* Advise clients on cash management, working capital optimization, and debt structuring

  



Client Reporting \& Communication
* Prepare and present monthly or quarterly financial reporting packages to client owners, leadership teams and board of directors
* Translate complex financial data into clear, actionable insights for non\-financial stakeholders
* Lead recurring financial review meetings with clients, identifying trends, risks and opportunities
* Provide ad hoc financial analysis and scenario planning as client needs arise

  



Coordination with Firm Service Teams
* Collaborate with the firm’s CAS staff to ensure accuracy and timeliness of client financials
* Partner with tax professionals to align advisory recommendations with tax planning strategies
* Coordinate with payroll team and third\-party payroll providers on compensation analysis, payroll tax compliance, and benefits planning
* Serve as the escalation point for complex accounting or financial questions from the CAS team

  



Transaction \& Growth Advisory
* Support clients through major financial events including M\&A due diligence, business valuations and exit planning
* Assist with loan applications, banking relationships and lender reporting requirements
* Guide clients through succession planning and ownership transitions
* Evaluate and advise on new business opportunities, market expansion and strategic partnerships

  



Practice Development \& Client Retention
* Identify opportunities to expand advisory services within existing client relationships
* Contribute to the development of the firm’s fractional CFO service offering, including engagement templates, pricing structures and deliverable standards
* Participate in client proposals, prospect meetings and thought leadership efforts to support firm growth
* Provide feedback to CAS leadership on client needs and service gaps

  


**Required Skills/Abilities:**
* Expertise in financial management in a variety of industries, strategic planning, and corporate finance.
* Proficient in tax law, GAAP, and IRS regulations.
* Proven experience delivering services to multiple clients simultaneously
* Strong command of financial modeling, forecasting, budgeting, and data\-driven decision\-making
* Ability to manage a portfolio of client relationships and adapt communication style to diverse audiences
* Excellent executive presence with the ability to advise business owners, C\-suite executives, and boards
* Familiarity with CAS workflows, monthly close processes, and cloud\-based accounting platforms
* Proficiency in using financial software, ERP systems, and Microsoft Office, as well as familiarity with accounting\-specific software.
* Strong analytical, negotiation, and communication skills.
* Ability to lead, inspire, and manage teams effectively.

  


**Education and Experience:**
* Certified Public Accountant (CPA) or similar qualifications is highly preferred.
* 10\+ years of progressive financial leadership experience, with at least 3 years in a CFO, VP of Finance, or Controller capacity

title: Accounting Operations Manager
description:
**Accounting Operations Manager**

*Remote \| Full\-Time or Part\-Time \| US\-Based or Fluent English Required*

**About The Chic CFO**

The Chic CFO is a fractional CFO and bookkeeping firm that helps small business owners — especially cleaning company owners — take control of their finances. We don't just categorize transactions. We deliver custom financial dashboards, clear reporting, and the kind of financial clarity that helps our clients know exactly what to pay themselves, eliminate tax surprises, and design the life they actually want.

We're a small, high\-performing remote team and we're growing. We're looking for a sharp, client\-facing Accounting Operations Manager to join us and take ownership of both our client relationships and our bookkeeping team's day\-to\-day performance.

**The Role**

This is not a traditional bookkeeping position. You will be the operational and client\-facing backbone of The Chic CFO. You'll lead new client onboarding, serve as the primary point of contact for our clients, manage and quality\-check our bookkeeping team's work, and make sure every deliverable goes out accurately and on time.

You need to be as comfortable on a client call as you are inside QuickBooks Online. You are organized, proactive, and you hold yourself and others to a high standard.

**What You'll Be Responsible For**

**Client Relationship Management**

* Lead new client onboarding calls and walk clients through our process from day one
* Serve as the primary point of contact for ongoing client communication
* Respond to client questions professionally and in a timely manner
* Build strong relationships that reduce churn and increase client satisfaction

**Bookkeeping Team Oversight**

* Manage and support our bookkeeping team, ensuring all work meets our quality standards
* Review completed bookkeeping work before it goes to clients
* Identify errors, inconsistencies, or missing items and ensure they are corrected
* Hold the team accountable to deadlines and deliverable timelines
* Provide feedback and direction to bookkeepers as needed

**Operations \& Deliverables**

* Own the client deliverables calendar and ensure nothing falls through the cracks
* Monitor the status of all active client accounts on an ongoing basis
* Step in to assist with bookkeeping when workload requires it
* Support process improvement initiatives as the team grows

**What We're Looking For**

**Must\-Haves**

* 3\+ years of bookkeeping or accounting experience
* Proficient in QuickBooks Online (QBO certification is a strong plus)
* Experience in a client\-facing role — you are comfortable on the phone and over video
* Strong written and verbal English communication skills
* Experience reviewing or overseeing another person's work
* Highly organized with excellent follow\-through
* Able to work US business hours (Eastern or Central time)

**Nice\-to\-Haves**

* Experience working at a bookkeeping firm, accounting agency, or fractional CFO company
* Familiarity with Dubsado, Looker Studio, Asana, or similar tools
* Experience working with service\-based small businesses (cleaning companies a major plus)
* Comfortable building or reviewing financial reports and dashboards

**Compensation**

Compensation is based on experience and location. This role starts at approximately 30 hours per week with the potential to grow to full\-time as the business scales.

**How to Apply**

To be considered, please submit the following:

* A 60–90 second Loom video introducing yourself and briefly describing your bookkeeping and client\-facing experience
* Your resume
* A note on your current availability and preferred hours

***Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed.***

We are excited to find the right person to grow with us. If you are detail\-oriented, love working with clients, and want to be part of a brand that is genuinely changing how small business owners relate to their money — we would love to hear from you.

*thechicCFO.com*

Work Location: Remote

title: Accounting Manager - Web3 Practice
description:
**Join the Rapidly Growing Team at Attivo Partners!**



Are you dedicated to excellence and eager to advance your career? Passionate about technology and startups? Excited to work with some of the most innovative early\-stage companies in the world? If so, Attivo Partners is the place for you!


**Why Attivo?**



Attivo Partners is a fast\-growing full\-service finance and accounting consulting firm headquartered in San Francisco, with a presence in major startup hubs across the US. Our remote teams specialize in providing fractional CFO and accounting services to venture\-backed emerging growth clients, supporting their journey from inception to over $50M in revenue.


**What We Offer:**


* **Collaborative Environment:** Work alongside a talented team with extensive experience in venture\-backed companies. At Attivo, we believe in teamwork and value the unique insights and creativity each member brings.
* **Culture of Growth:** We thrive on the challenges presented by diverse and exciting clients, offering unparalleled learning and professional development opportunities.
* **Mentorship and Training:** We are committed to nurturing talent and providing ongoing mentoring and training to help you grow personally and professionally.
* **Flexibility:** We understand the importance of balance and are dedicated to maintaining a culture that supports your career and personal life.
  


**Our Values:**


* **Lasting Relationships:** We build solid and enduring connections with our clients and team members.
* **Data\-Driven Insights:** We leverage data to drive intelligent decisions and impactful results.
* **Growth\-Focused Mindset:** We embrace opportunities for continuous improvement and innovation.
* **Collaboration\-First:** We prioritize teamwork and believe the best solutions come from working together.
* **Culture\-Conscious:** We foster a supportive and inclusive culture where everyone can thrive.


Join us at Attivo Partners and be part of a team that's shaping the future of finance and accounting for startups. Let's grow together!


**Role Overview**



The Accounting Manager position is a hands\-on role, responsible for accounting functions for multiple clients that include full\-cycle month\-end close, accounts receivable and payable, payroll, and tax compliance. The Accounting Manager is responsible for managing the general on\-chain and off\-chain accounting for clients, assisting with the creation of financial statements and management reporting, managing the organization's cash, and assisting with the creation of departmental budgets and forecasts. Experience with developing and establishing policies and procedures within a corporation is highly preferred. The ideal candidate has worked at or with early\-stage start\-ups.



The position location is flexible/work from home, with preference given to candidates in the following cities where Attivo has an employee presence: New York City, Austin, and Boston. Candidates in other locations are welcome to apply. Some travel to in\-person company events is required, approximately 2\-3 weeks out of the year. In\-person onboarding training will take place within the first 3 months of employment (locations vary).


**Responsibilities**


* Produce monthly financial statements and budget vs actual analysis
* Assist with financial modeling and forecasting for clients
* Utilize on\-chain accounting tools to streamline processes and improve data accuracy
* Manage Accounts Receivable \- which includes invoicing, collections, reviewing contracts
* Manage Accounts Payable \- which includes ensuring accurate entry, timely payment, and compliance with company policies
* Manage Token Accounting \- which includes token warrants and token compensation
* Process employee onboarding, payroll, and terminations
* Mentor and train future team members


**Requirements**


* Solid understanding of GAAP, CPA or CFA preferred
* Advanced\+ Excel and ability to manipulate larger data sets; experience with SQL a \+
* Experience with crypto accounting software (e.g. Bitwave, Integral) and reconciliation of on\-chain transactions
* Experience with standard software solutions: QuickBooks Online, Xero, NetSuite, Campfire, Brex, Ramp, Rippling, Gusto, Rillet, Carta
* Excellent written and verbal communication, comfortable with basic Web3 vocabulary
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities
* Independent judgment and decision\-making
* Ability to manage multiple projects/priorities at a time
* Professional curiosity \& creativity for Web3 innovation and financial transformation
* Passionate desire to learn \& for continuous improvement


**Education \& Experience**


* Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance preferred. CPA or CFA is a plus
* 6\+ years of progressive accounting experience with at least 2 years in a supervisory role; multi\-client experience highly preferred
* 2\+ years of recent experience at a Web3 company or crypto native. If you do not have professional experience, please share the ecosystem within blockchain \& crypto that you are excited about and why, in your resume or in a short cover memo.
* Experience working in a fast\-paced technology, or professional services environment
### **Professional Attributes**


* Professional integrity
* High energy and enthusiasm, with a strong commitment to exceeding client expectations
* Flexibility and openness to work on a variety of assignments, industries, and roles
* Ability to learn quickly, make an immediate impact, and provide value\-added service
* Strong ability to connect and build relationships with clients, firm leadership, \& peers
* Confidence in professional aptitude and ability to effectively convey skills, knowledge, and qualifications to clients
* Team player with a strong desire to be an active, long\-term participant in Attivo's growth
* Entrepreneurial spirit and passion for startups and early\-stage growth companies

### **Salary Range**



Attivo offers a competitive base plus bonus, and opportunities for advancement. The starting salary range for Accounting Manager is approximately $110,000 to $140,000 per year plus bonus.

  


### **Benefits**


Benefits for full\-time employees include: Health, Dental, and Vision Insurance (100% coverage for employees on base plan, 70% coverage for additional family members), participation in Flexible Spending Accounts and 401(k), 3 weeks PTO, voluntary Life Insurance \& Disability plans, and work\-from\-home allowance. Primary caregivers and others requiring a flexible work schedule or reduced hours are encouraged to apply; benefits may be prorated for part\-time hires.
### **Final Notes**


Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

Attivo Partners is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to a diverse workplace. People from diverse racial, ethnic and cultural backgrounds, women, LGBTQ\+ individuals, and persons with disabilities are highly encouraged to apply.

Attivo Partners will never request payment, sensitive personal information, or unsolicited account access at any stage of the hiring process. All legitimate communication from our team will come \*exclusively from an @attivopartners.com email address. If you receive a message claiming to be from Attivo that does not use this domain \- or if something feels unfamiliar \- please disregard the communication and report it to us at Recruiting@Attivopartners.com.

title: Virtual/Fractional CFO
description:
SPRCHRGR (pronounced "supercharger") is a professional services consultancy providing highly skilled team members across a variety of complementary disciplines. We like to say, "cut out the vowels, get there faster!" because we prioritize creativity, efficiency, and performance in our work, and we have fun doing it.



Our exceptional staff \+ innovative approach set us apart from stuffy traditional accountancies and commoditized outsourced bookkeeping agencies. We think differently about our work supporting high\-growth startups, forward\-thinking SMBs, and nonprofits located all across the US.



We specialize in **cutting\-edge technology** and partner with dynamic businesses across diverse industries. Our team culture emphasizes **collaboration**, **learning**, and **process\-driven efficiency**.

**Accepting Applications**



We are looking for stellar Virtual CFOs who want to support an interesting mix of businesses while building and managing strong client relationships. This opportunity is for you if you're ready to take on new challenges and work with clients ranging from cutting\-edge startups to private equity funds and everything in between.



If you are an experienced accounting \& finance professional with an impressive ability to think on your feet, learn quickly, and solve problems \- we want you!



Our Virtual CFOs are critical members of our clients' finance department, bringing financial planning \& analysis and leadership expertise to drive business intelligence and strategy for growing businesses. They build financial forecast models, annual budgets, operating plans, profitability \& variance analyses, and cash flow models. They lead and work on a broad variety of special projects such as KPI analyses, cohort analyses, inventory demand planning, due diligence support, and M\&A support. Virtual CFOs are also often involved as functional analysts in custom software development work we specialize in to supercharge our client's back office.



This position directs the work of FP\&A Managers and Sr. Financial Analysts on our staff to provide hands\-on mentoring while benefitting from a team\-based approach to divide and conquer on each work assignment. This is a position that is proactive and solution\-oriented, supporting clients in the fast\-paced world of startups and high\-growth SMBs. Virtual CFOs have the opportunity to increase their earnings with us through profit\-sharing bonuses and other executive compensation awards.


**Responsibilities**


* Mentor and direct the work of FP\&A Managers and Sr. Financial Analysts on our staff
* Responsible for overall fiscal operations: direct our dedicated accounting teams on their functions supporting the full monthly accounting cycle of client businesses
* Supervise client engagements and occasionally direct the client's own internal accounting \& finance team
* Maintain useful relationships with outside partners and vendors engaged with each client business
* Develop short\-range and long\-range financial plans to achieve established corporate goals and maximize client profitability
* Provide ongoing financial analysis and modeling to a range of company leaders in support of key business decisions
* Present timely and valid interpretation of operating results to board of directors and senior management team of clients
* Provide sound leadership in complying with financial and legal requirements
* Attend client board meetings (virtually)
* Support clients with investor capital raise activities and bank debt negotiations
* Prepare for and support buy\-side and sell\-side due diligence for client businesses
* Analyze current market trends and competition of clients' industries
* Collaborate with others to solve ad\-hoc technical finance and accounting challenges
* Assist with systems implementations and custom software solutions


**Qualifications**


* 12\+ years in a financial role with at least 8\+ years as a finance leader
* Strong understanding of equity and debt financing
* familiarity with managing startup equity cap tables
* Proven track record in successfully supporting growth using strong analytical skills and effective financial modeling and tracking
* Experience leading financial budgeting and financial planning process
* Ability to oversee and review corporate accounting functions
* Solid understanding of financial compliance requirements and experience implementing and managing financial policies
* Proactive and client\-centric attitude
* Fast learner w/ability to effectively prioritize and manage time
* Hungry for experience in fast\-paced, dynamic business environments


**Proficient in**


* Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, PowerPoint, learning new software


**Compensation**


* Competitive Pay
* 401K w/ up to 4% match (no vesting)
* Discretionary Bonus Program
* 100% remote, always
* Customizable benefit options
* The work is performed M\-F, between 8 AM to 5 PM PST hours
* We can provide you with a laptop and necessary hardware/software

  

**Application Notice**



At SPRCHRGR, we value fairness, clarity, and attention to detail in our hiring process. To ensure an equitable process, we ask that you submit your application only through the official posting for this role.


* Do not contact our hiring managers, team members, or leadership directly (via personal email, LinkedIn, or other channels). Applications received in this way will be automatically disqualified.
* All questions or inquiries regarding your application should be directed to our recruiting team at recruiter@sprchrgr.com.


**Notice to External Recruiters**  

Please do not send unsolicited candidate submissions to our team. Our system automatically blocks domains that repeatedly send unrequested outreach.

  


**WHO WE ARE**


**Ethical**



We do good. We act with integrity, earning trust instead of asking for it, and we do what is right even when it isn't easy. We are transparent, promptly sharing information and feedback directly and freely with all relevant stakeholders.

  


**Positive**



We love what we do. It shows in the positive and constructive attitude we bring every day. We realize work is more than just output; it's about the life experiences we share along the way.

  


**Passionate**



We care. A lot. We expect high performance of ourselves and our teammates. We constantly look to innovate and improve. We play to win and keep raising the bar to deliver results.

  


**Professional**



We are one team, focused on the "we" rather than the "I." We're consistently organized and accountable. Our work speaks for itself and stands the test of time.

  


**Inclusive**



By design, our team members believe, act, feel, and look differently from each other. We foster a sense of belonging and respect for every individual and we expect the same from our clients and partners.

  

**HOW WE OPERATE**


**We focus on what matters**



We work efficiently but don't cut corners or compromise on best practices. We prioritize long\-term value over short\-term gains. We value simplicity over complexity.

  


**We reward it**



We reward the effort of our peers with matched enthusiasm. We share the spotlight and credit generously. We celebrate our successes, even the small ones.

  


**We own it**



We take pride in our work. We see projects through from start to finish. We continuously update process documentation and fix things we find broken along the way.

  


**We're a service\-first organization**



We exemplify a customer\-first mindset across everything we do internally \& externally, always learning from our colleagues, partners, and clients. While the customer isn't always right, we value their trust in us and strive to reciprocate value by anticipating their needs, priorities, and expectations.

title: Regional Manager
description:
**Job Title: Regional Manager**
===============================


(Primary Team Leader \& Revenue Owner)

**Reporting Structure**
-----------------------


Direct Reports (Upon Completion of The Transition Period)

* Client Coordinators
* NPs (With clinical oversight supported by CMO / MD)
* Nurses (With clinical oversight supported by NP/CMO)
* Wellness Experience Coordinators
* Marketing Manager

**Role Overview**
-----------------


The Regional Manager is the single\-threaded owner of performance across 3\+ locations, including two brick\-and\-mortar clinics, a fractional franchise model, and future partnership\-based expansions. This role oversees clinic operations, mobile services, and events business with full accountability for revenue growth, team performance, and operational execution.


This role exists at a critical inflection point as IVitamin scales its multi\-unit, multi\-model business, requiring a leader who can translate strategy into execution across locations, strengthening sales discipline, and building a high\-accountability culture that supports sustainable growth.


The Regional Manager is a highly hands\-on operator who leads from the front—coaching in real time, using data to drive decisions, and building scalable systems that improve conversion, retention, revenue per client, and overall profitability.

**WHO WE ARE**
--------------


At IVitamin, we believe that wellness is a journey defined by its ups and downs, and the only true way to enjoy the lifestyle you choose is to feel your best while you experience it.


Put simply: Feel your best, be your best, live your best life.  

  

Whether you’re a tireless mom, devoted athlete, busy entrepreneur, or wanderlust traveler – IVitamin is made up of real people looking to feel better and find a healthy life balance with IV hydration therapy while putting a priority on health and wellness.

**Our WHY**
-----------


At IVitamin, our North Star is simple but powerful: promoting health, energy, vitality, and the joy of feeling your best. This guiding beacon is at the core of everything we do.

**Decision Authority \& Ownership**
-----------------------------------


The Regional Manager has full authority over day\-to\-day operations across all assigned locations, including staffing, scheduling, sales execution, performance management, and oversight of local marketing initiatives that drive traffic, conversion, and membership growth. This role is empowered to make real\-time decisions in the best interest of the business and is trusted to operate autonomously within established budgets, SOPs, and strategic priorities set by leadership.

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------


Revenue \& Sales Execution

* Own sales performance across brick\-and\-mortar clinics, mobile services, and events
* Drive measurable growth in conversion rates, membership adoption, customer retention, and revenue per visit
* Partner on and execute brick\-and\-mortar marketing strategies that drive local acquisition, increase foot traffic, and improve in\-clinic conversion and membership growth.
* Ensure sales processes are consistently followed by Client Coordinators and clinical staff
* Coach team members on consult conversations, education\-based selling, and conversion
* Monitor performance against sales targets, ensure team accountability


Team Leadership \& Performance Management

* Serve as the primary team leader for clinic and mobile teams
* Coach managers and frontline teams through structured check\-ins, role\-play, and performance conversations
* Address performance issues promptly and professionally
* Make hiring, corrective action, and termination decisions as needed
* Build a culture of accountability, professionalism, and client\-focused service


Operational Execution \& SOP Adherence

* Ensure consistent execution of SOPs across all locations and service channels
* Partner with leadership to standardize processes for operational efficiency and multi\-unit scalability
* Observe staff on the floor and provide real\-time feedback
* Ensure high\-quality, safe, and compliant service delivery in coordination with NP and CMO


Client Experience

* Own the end\-to\-end client experience across all locations and service lines
* Resolve escalated client concerns with professionalism and urgency
* Ensure the team delivers a consistent, high\-quality experience aligned with the IVitamin brand
* Implement systems that improve client satisfaction, rebooking, and lifetime value


Leadership \& Cross\-Functional Collaboration

* Partner closely with the cross\-functional leaders to translate company strategy into clear, executable actions at the location level
* Provide insights from the field on performance, challenges, and opportunities to inform business decisions and growth strategy
* Execute initiatives and priorities set by leadership
* Contribute to the evolution of systems, playbooks, and training that support long\-term scalability

**What This Role Is Not**
-------------------------

* This is not a purely administrative or desk\-based management role
* This is not a passive oversight position
* This role requires presence, leadership, and real\-time decision\-making

**What Success Looks Like**
---------------------------

* Sales targets are consistently met or exceeded
* Measurable improvements in conversion, retention, and average ticket value
* Teams are highly engaged, coached consistently, and performing at a high level
* SOPs are followed in real\-world execution, not just on paper
* Client experience is consistent, professional, and high\-quality
* Issues are addressed quickly, directly, and constructively
* New locations and business models launch smoothly with strong performance
* Data is actively used to drive decisions and continuous improvement

**Growth Opportunity**
----------------------


This role is designed for a high\-impact operator who thrives in scale environments. As IVitamin continues to expand, this position will evolve into broader regional or market leadership based on performance.

**Qualifications**
------------------

* 5\+ years of senior leadership experience (Regional Manager, General Manager, Director\-level) in a multi\-location environment
* Experience operating within a wellness, healthcare, hospitality or retail environment strongly preferred. RN license or clinical background is a significant plus.
* Proven track record of driving measurable revenue growth and customer service performance, including improving conversion, retention, and overall profitability.
* Strong background in sales strategy, membership/subscription models, or service\-based revenue environments.
* Strong understanding and hands\-on experience in marketing for brick\-and\-mortar locations, including local acquisition, community engagement, and in\-clinic conversion strategies.
* Highly data\-driven with experience using KPIs and performance metrics to inform decisions and drive outcomes.
* Demonstrated success building and developing high\-performing teams, including mentoring leaders and scaling teams across multiple locations.
* Experience creating or implementing training systems, playbooks, or performance frameworks that improve execution.
* Strong operational discipline with the ability to implement and scale systems, enforce SOP adherence, and drive consistent execution across locations.
* Experience in wellness, healthcare, hospitality, or retail environments strongly preferred; clinical exposure is a plus (RN or clinical background a bonus).
* High level of emotional intelligence and leadership maturity, including experience with leadership and personal development frameworks (e.g., Four Agreements, Enneagram, or similar).
* Strong personal accountability with the ability to assess gaps objectively and implement corrective action.
* Must be comfortable in a fast\-paced, hands\-on, in\-person leadership role across multiple locations, including weekends as needed.

**Compensation \& Performance Incentives**
------------------------------------------

* Target base salary range: $100,000\-120,000
* This role includes a performance\-based bonus tied to clinic revenue growth, sales conversion, and overall business performance. High performers should expect meaningful upside tied directly to results, not discretionary or subjective metrics.

**How to Apply**
----------------


In addition to applying with your resume please email a Loom video to sroman@ivitaminatx.com with the subject line: ‘General Manager Application – \[Your Name]' and answer the following two questions:* Describe a time when you were responsible for improving sales or customer service performance. What strategies did you implement, and what measurable impact did they have?
* How do you balance high\-accountability leadership with a servant leadership mindset? Please share an example of how you’ve held strong standards while building trust and engagement with your team.

title: Fractional CMO
description:
**Position Title:**Fractional CMO – Growth \& Brand Strategy

**Location:**Southern California

**Wage Scale:**$10,000 USD to $12,000 USD

 **About Us:**


True Legacy Homes is a rapidly growing residential real estate investment company specializing in home renovation, estate sale services, and life transition solutions. We purchase homes directly for cash, thoughtfully renovate them, and resell them—bringing new life to properties while simplifying the selling process for homeowners.


We’re passionate about transforming both homes and lives. Whether guiding families through estate sales, providing senior transition support, or offering compassionate, tailored solutions during major life changes, our team leads with professionalism, transparency, and care.

  

As a trusted leader in estate sales and senior transition services, we’re redefining how families navigate these moments. With plans for national expansion, we’re seeking driven, results\-driven professionals with an entrepreneurial mindset to help shape the future of life transition services while building a meaningful career.


We are seeking a strategic and entrepreneurial Fractional Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) to partner closely with the CEO and leadership team. This role is designed for a highly experienced marketing leader who can translate insights, ideas, and business objectives into clear, actionable marketing strategies that drive growth.

  

The ideal candidate is both a strong listener and a visionary thinker—someone who can synthesize what we know about our business, customers, and market into compelling marketing initiatives. This individual will play a critical role in shaping our brand(s), expanding beyond traditional paid advertising, and building scalable marketing systems that support long\-term success.

 **Duties and Responsibilities:**

* Develop and implement comprehensive marketing strategies that drive brand awareness, customer acquisition, and revenue growth.
* Translate business insights and leadership input into clear, effective marketing plans and campaigns.
* Build and execute integrated marketing campaigns beyond paid advertising and PPC, including organic, content, brand, and lifecycle marketing.
* Lead the development and evolution of one or more company brands, including positioning, messaging, and identity.
* Partner closely with the CEO to align marketing initiatives with overall company vision and objectives.
* Establish and optimize marketing processes, tools, and performance metrics to ensure efficiency and ROI.

 **Required Qualifications:**

* Proven experience in a senior marketing leadership role (CMO, VP of Marketing, or equivalent).
* Strong strategic thinking with the ability to turn ideas and insights into actionable plans.
* Demonstrated success in building brands and executing multi\-channel marketing strategies.
* Entrepreneurial mindset with a hands\-on, results\-driven approach.
* Excellent listening, communication, and collaboration skills.
* Experience working in a fractional or consulting capacity is preferred.

 **Location Consideration:**

**The position is remote. While candidates based in Southern California are preferred, we will also consider applicants located within the Pacific or Central Time Zones.**

 **How to Apply:**


Please submit your resume and cover letter outlining your relevant experience and qualifications to Daniela.Luarca@truelegacyhomes.com. We look forward to hearing from you!

 ***True Legacy Homes is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.***

This is a high\-impact executive role for a physician leader who is energized by **population health, risk\-based models, and integrated care delivery**. The CMO will partner closely with executive leadership, operations, and clinical teams to drive initiatives that improve quality, outcomes, and performance across the enterprise.



This role offers a unique opportunity to help shape the future of long\-term care within a value\-based framework and to make a meaningful impact on the care of a large, medically complex population.

* Medical Leadership for **ISNP and value\-based care initiatives.**
* Driving quality improvement, utilization management, and outcomes optimization.

* **Experience with ISNPs, Medicare Advantage, or value\-based care** strongly preferred.

Compassion, Accountability, Relationships and Excellence are the core values for American Senior Communities. description:

Our proprietary **AudienceXLerator™** program combines media buying technology with personalized creative and high\-propensity data to efficiently market an entire calendar of events — sports, the arts, museums, concerts, and more.
