title: Financial Analyst
description:
**About Ampleo**

**Cross\-Functional Leadership and Execution to Fuel Growth**

**We provide the expertise to accelerate your business at every stage and in every function.**

**Since 1996, Amplēo has helped thousands of businesses grow through on\-demand finance leadership, starting with fractional CFOs and outsourced controllers. What began as strategic financial support has evolved into a complete suite of cross\-functional services.**

**Today, Amplēo delivers embedded expertise in finance, HR, marketing, turnaround and restructuring, sales tax, and business valuation. Our professionals integrate directly into your team to provide both strategic insight and hands\-on execution, helping you achieve meaningful results faster.**

**We don’t just advise. We help you move, build, and win.**

 **Position Summary**


This is a high\-intensity, execution focused role embedded in live engagements. You will work alongside senior professionals and directly with client CFOs, treasurers, and operations teams to build the financial infrastructure needed to navigate distressed situations. Engagements span out\-of\-court workouts, Chapter 11 proceedings, covenant defaults, operational restructurings, and lender advisory assignments. No two situations are the same speed, accuracy, and sound judgment under pressure are the core requirements.

 **Key Responsibilities**


Cash \& liquidity management

* Build and maintain 13\-week cash flow (TWCF) models for distressed companies, updated weekly with actuals vs. forecast variance analysis
* Develop rolling liquidity forecasts and identify near\-term cash constraints
* Support daily cash monitoring and disbursement prioritization during acute liquidity crises
* Prepare cash collateral reporting for court filings and lender updates


Financial modeling \& analysis

* Construct integrated three\-statement models (P\&L, balance sheet, cash flow) for restructuring scenario analysis
* Build and stress\-test recovery analyses, waterfall models, and enterprise valuations under distressed assumptions
* Develop debt capacity and capital structure analyses to evaluate restructuring alternatives
* Prepare sensitivity and scenario analyses to support negotiation strategies


Operational \& performance improvement

* Analyze operating cost structures to identify EBITDA improvement and cash conservation opportunities
* Support development of operational restructuring plans including headcount, vendor, and facility rationalization
* Assist with working capital optimization — inventory, A/R, and A/P analysis
* Track KPIs and prepare management reporting packages for client leadership


Stakeholder \& process support

* Prepare materials for lender, creditor committee, and board presentations
* Support due diligence requests from lenders, advisors, and potential investors
* Assist in drafting first\-day motions, DIP budgets, and plan of reorganization financial projections
* Coordinate with legal counsel, investment bankers, and claims agents on data and deliverable requests

### **Qualifications**

* 2–5 years of experience in investment banking, restructuring advisory, Big 4 transaction services, corporate finance, or a related field
* Advanced Excel proficiency complex model builds, dynamic linking, scenario management
* Demonstrated experience building 13\-week cash flow models or integrated financial models from scratch
* Strong command of accounting fundamentals and financial statement analysis
* Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting, economics, or related discipline
* Ability to work in a fast\-moving, ambiguous environment with tight deadlines and shifting priorities

**Preferred**

* Direct restructuring or distressed experience — in\-house, advisory, or lender\-side
* Familiarity with Chapter 11 processes, first\-day relief, DIP financing, and plan confirmation
* Experience working in a client\-facing or on\-site advisory context
* CPA, CFA, CTP, CFE or CIRA in progress or completed
* Exposure to bankruptcy court reporting requirements (MOR, DIP budget variance, cash collateral reporting)
* Proficiency in PowerPoint for executive\-level presentation development

 **Benefits**

* Flexible PTO
* Medical, dental, and vision insurance with FSA options
* Company\-paid life insurance
* Paid parental leave
* 401(k) with company match
* Professional development opportunities
* 12 paid holidays off

title: Business Development Manager
description:
**\*Please no outside recruiting agencies as we do all recruiting in\-house\***




**Spartan Shield Solutions** is a rapidly growing veteran\-owned Fractional CFO and Outsourced Accounting firm based in Arlington, VA. We serve organizations across all industries, with a focus on government contractors. Spartan Shield works closely with its clients to improve operations, productivity and organization while meeting compliance requirements. We do this through increasing access to up\-to\-date financial information, reducing inefficiencies and freeing senior management to focus more on revenue generating activities.


Spartan Shield is seeking a vibrant, driven individual to help the continued success of the company in a **Business Development Manager** capacity. The **Business Development Manager** will work closely with the CEO and be instrumental in generating new business, participating in networking events and conferences, and building lasting relationships with potential clients. Successful candidates will have an outgoing personality, is comfortable speaking to many different personalities, and enjoys networking at a variety of events.




A quality work\-life balance is a top priority; therefore, we provide a generous PTO policy and telecommuting options. Spartan Shield offers competitive salaries and a comprehensive benefits package. Must be local to the DMV (DC, Maryland, Virginia) area as this role is a remote role with travel to multiple local events in the DMV area weekly. Out of town travel will be around 10 times per year.




**QUALIFICATIONS**

* A Bachelor's degree preferred
* Minimum of 5\+ year of business development and prospecting new clients
* Supervisory experience required
* Background in Government Contractor experience a plus
* Ability to multitask and possess strong customer interaction skills
* Proficiency in computer systems including Microsoft applications and other standard office applications
* Must have the ability to solve problems, apply new information, learn new skills, and possess critical thinking
* Excellent written and oral communication skills
* Must be eligible to work in the United States and not require sponsorship now or in the future




**RESPONSIBILITIES**

* Lead and grow the Business Development team
* Provide superior customer service
* Attend multiple networking events and conferences on a weekly basis
* Develop a thorough working knowledge of company services and pricing structures
* Research and confirm leads, initiate outreach, and build meaningful engagement with perspective clients
* Overcome objections and secure meetings with Executives of prospective clients
* Supporting the Director of Business Development in understanding prospective clients' needs and proposing appropriate service solutions
* Prepare sales presentations, proposals, and contract agreements
* Travel to local networking events and clients' meetings weekly
* Travel out of town about 10 times per year




**Benefits:**

* Simple IRA and IRA matching
* STD/LTD/Life Insurance
* Health insurance
* Vision insurance
* Dental insurance
* Annual performance bonus
* Additional bonus opportunities
* Flexible spending account
* Paid time off




EOE M/F/D/V/SO

title: Sales Assistant
description:
**About Northern Jet:**


Personal. Private. Authentic. Northern Jet is a private aviation company with over 31 years of experience, offering personalized luxury travel with a focus on safety, convenience, and quality. We operate a fleet of 40\+ jets and hold an ARGUS Platinum safety rating. Northern Jet’s mission is to deliver seamless, client\-focused travel experiences.

**About this Role:**  

The Sales Assistant at Northern Jet is an entry\-level opportunity for individuals looking to break into private aviation, luxury sales, and high\-value relationship building.


This is a base pay plus commission role designed for someone who wants to learn how private jet sales work by being directly involved in lead generation, prospecting, and supporting real deals across Jet Card, Fractional Jet Ownership, and Aircraft Management.


You will work alongside experienced sales professionals and gain exposure to high\-net\-worth clients, strategic partnerships, and the full sales cycle within private aviation.

**Location:** Orlando, Florida

**Schedule:** Monday\-Friday, in office

**Key Duties and Responsibilities**

**Lead Generation and Prospecting**

* Research and build lists of qualified prospects including business owners, executives, and high\-net\-worth individuals
* Identify individuals who travel frequently between the Midwest and Florida
* Utilize LinkedIn, networking events, and outbound outreach to generate interest

**Outreach and Relationship Building**

* Initiate conversations through cold calls, email, LinkedIn, and in\-person networking
* Introduce Northern Jet in a professional way
* Build familiarity and trust with potential clients and partners

**Pipeline Development**

* Qualify prospects and pass strong opportunities to senior sales team members
* Track all activity in CRM
* Ensure all leads are followed up with and properly categorized

**Event and Experience Support**

* Assist with events such as golf outings, private hangar events, and luxury experiences
* Help identify and invite qualified prospects
* Engage with attendees and support relationship building efforts

**Sales Support Exposure**

* Sit in on discovery calls and sales meetings when appropriate
* Learn how proposals are structured and presented
* Gain understanding of Jet Cards, Fractional Ownership, and Aircraft Management

 **Minimum Qualifications**

* Highly motivated, curious, and coachable\[AW1]
* Strong communication skills
* Comfortable networking and starting conversations
* Interested in luxury sales, aviation, or business development
* Willing to learn and put in consistent effort
* No prior aviation experience required

**Compensation Structure**
--------------------------


This is a position designed to reward performance and initiative.


Additional compensation opportunities include:

* Base pay \+ commission
* Commission participation on closed deals
* Potential growth into a full\-time sales role with higher earning potential
 **Why This Role Exists:**


Private aviation is a relationship\-driven industry where access and consistency create opportunity.  

This role is built to develop future sales talent by focusing on the most valuable skill in the business: generating and qualifying high\-quality opportunities.  

  

You will learn how to:

* Identify and connect with qualified prospects
* Build relationships within luxury and business networks
* Support real sales conversations and transactions
* Understand how private aviation products are sold and structured

 **How This Role Fits Into The Sales Team**

* Business Development and focus on building relationships and generating opportunities
* Aviation Advisors focus on closing deals
* Sales Assistant focuses on **creating and feeding the pipeline**

  

 This role is the starting point of the entire sales engine.

**Application Process:**  

At Northern Jet, we are not just hiring resumes. We are identifying individuals who can communicate, connect, and represent our brand in front of high\-level clients.


As part of the application process, all candidates are required to submit a short video introduction.


This allows us to evaluate presence, communication skills, and professionalism, which are critical in private aviation sales.

**STEP 1: Initial Application**


Submit your application to the job posting.

**STEP 2: Video Submission (Required)**


Record a **2 to 3 minute video** of yourself on camera and email via a shareable link (Google Drive, Dropbox, or Loom) to recruiting@northernjet.com along with your resume.

**In your video, please cover:**

* **Introduction**  

 Who you are and your background
* **Why Northern Jet**  

 Why you are interested in private aviation and this opportunity
* **Why You**  

 What makes you a strong fit for a commission\-based, relationship\-driven role
* **Proof of Initiative**  

 Share an example of a time you took initiative, built a relationship, or created an opportunity

**Why Northern Jet?**

  

* Work with a leading private aviation company focused on exclusive experiences.
* Collaborate with a dynamic marketing team in a fast\-growing industry.
* Opportunity to make a meaningful impact on the company’s growth and brand presence.
* Exposure to luxury sales strategies and high\-net\-worth clientele engagement.

**Your Career. Your Benefits. Your Northern Jet Advantage.**  

 At Northern Jet, we’re committed to taking care of the people who keep us flying. We offer a wide range of valuable benefits, many provided at no cost to support your health, financial stability, and overall quality of life. Our benefits are designed to help you thrive both professionally and personally, so you can focus on delivering excellence in the skies and enjoying life on the ground. Our comprehensive benefits include:

* **80% Company\-Paid Premiums for Medical, Dental, and Vision** coverage with access to nationwide provider networks
* **Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA)** for healthcare and dependent care
* **Health Savings Account (HSA)** option with tax\-advantaged savings
* **Wellness Programs \& Discounts** including LifeMart and exclusive ID90 Travel deals
* **Paid Time Off** starting at 2 weeks PTO plus 1 week of paid sick time annually
* **Life and Accidental Death \& Dismemberment Insurance** (company\-paid)
* **Voluntary Benefits** including accident, critical illness, identity theft protection, and hospital indemnity plans
* **401(k) plan** with a 3% company match
* **Short\- and Long\-Term Disability** (company\-paid)
* **Additional Lifestyle Perks** and travel discounts designed for the aviation industry


As part of our commitment to safety and security, all candidates are required to complete pre\-employment drug screening and background checks. Northern Jet also participates in the E\-Verify program to confirm employment eligibility for all new hires.

title: Leadership Development – Remote Partnership / Fractional
description:
**A Strategic Partnership for Experienced Leadership Professionals**

If you are an established leadership consultant, executive coach, or senior executive navigating a professional transition, you are likely not looking for another traditional role. You already bring credibility, influence, and professional judgment shaped by real responsibility.

This is an independent Strategic Partnership that is designed for experienced professionals who are prepared to deploy a proven leadership system rather than spend months, or years, building curriculum, infrastructure, and delivery models from scratch.

Whether you have recently exited a corporate role, are entering a fractional advisory phase, or are seeking to monetize decades of leadership experience, the challenge is the same: converting expertise into scalable, repeatable impact without reinventing everything yourself.

**The Opportunity**

Relationship Media, LLC is inviting a limited number of experienced professionals to deploy a field\-tested leadership system built around the *Be Like Tony \& Don’t Be Like Chad* framework.

Developed by a former S.W.A.T. commander with more than 20 years of real\-world leadership experience, the system addresses a growing breakdown in trust, communication, and accountability inside modern organizations.

This is not theoretical leadership training. It is a practical, example\-driven methodology designed to work across industries, organizational cultures, and leadership environments.

**The Commitment (Integration \& Execution)**

We are building a small, aligned group of Strategic Partners who are prepared to take ownership of their next chapter. This opportunity requires personal initiative, discipline, and follow\-through.

**What Is Provided**

* A complete, ready\-to\-deploy leadership system.
* A plug\-and\-play LMS and delivery infrastructure.
* Participant materials and administrative backend.
* Professional positioning and marketing assets.
* Dedicated one\-on\-one coaching provided after successful completion of the required system training to ensure proper positioning and confident deployment.

**What You Control**

* Your pace.
* Your schedule.
* Your professional relationships.
* Your results.

**Training**

* There is a mandatory training schedule and deadlines.
* Progress is self\-paced and driven by your initiative and ability to activate professional relationships.
* Full access to the leadership system, LMS, and Strategic Partner resources is provided immediately to support training and integration.
* Dedicated one\-on\-one coaching is made available after successful completion of the required system training to ensure proper positioning and responsible deployment of the framework.

**Income Model**

Compensation is performance\-based and tied directly to your ability to deploy the system within your existing relationships. Revenue is generated through the use and sale of the leadership training system.

Strategic Partners may leverage the established Relationship Media, LLC brand or inquire about advanced delivery options once aligned and established.

**This Is a Strong Fit If You:**

* Have a network of professionals that are responsible for making leadership training \& development decisions for their organization (HR, Operations, Business Owner, etc.)
* Are an experienced leadership trainer, consultant, or coach seeking a proven, example\-driven system to add to your practice.
* Are a professional who understands that relationships, not funnels, drives opportunity.
* Are Someone prepared to invest time, discipline, and focused effort into building momentum.

**This is NOT a Strong Fit If You:**

* Do not meet any of the above requirements.

**Important Note**

This is not a salaried role, a W\-2 position, or an opportunity where leads are provided. Strategic Partners are independent contractors responsible for generating opportunities through their own professional relationships and reputation.

**Next Step**

To ensure alignment, all interested candidates must complete a few short questions. This selective process is designed to confirm readiness, expectations, and fit before a private evaluation conversation is scheduled. Not everyone gets an invite to join our Strategic Partner team.

Pay: $1\.00 \- $500,000\.00 per year

Application Question(s):

* This Strategic Partner model requires you to personally initiate conversations and activate professional relationships to create opportunities. Do you understand and accept this responsibility?
* This opportunity is designed for professionals who value mastery over invention; those willing to study, internalize, and precisely implement a proven leadership system rather than reinvent one from scratch. It is not for those seeking shortcuts or customization before competence. Are you prepared to commit to the discipline, standards, and work required to become a certified coach and trainer within an established framework?
* Relationship Media, LLC does not provide leads, prospects, or booked appointments. Strategic Partners are responsible for generating opportunities through their own relationships and reputation. Do you understand and accept this responsibility?
* This opportunity is not an employment or salaried position. Strategic Partners are independent contractors and are not W\-2 employees. Do you understand and accept this structure?

Work Location: Remote

title: Account Executive - Fractional
description:
**Job Summary**  
We are hiring an **Account Executive** to help sell our software into the EV charging industry. This is a remote role focused on outbound sales, managing your own pipeline, and closing business.

You do **not** need to be an EV expert. We are looking for someone who wants to learn, sell, close deals, and make money. This role is a good fit for someone with some sales experience who wants more closing responsibility, better training, and the chance to build real deal volume on their resume.

This can work for a **fractional hire**, someone looking for a **part\-time role**, or someone trying to move from a **BDR/SDR role into a full\-cycle AE position**.

You will be selling **Rebate Database software** to a specific list of companies in the EV charging market. That includes manufacturers, equipment resellers, installers, and property owners. You will be working a defined target list of roughly **500 companies** and will be responsible for outbound outreach, follow\-up, pipeline management, negotiation, and closing.

This is a role for someone who wants to **make cash, get strong sales training, and close real business**. The founders will be hands\-on and will help train and support you along the way.

**What You’ll Do**

* Prospect a targeted list of companies in the EV charging industry
* Cold call, send emails, follow up, and book meetings
* Build lead lists and identify decision\-makers
* Manage your own pipeline from first outreach through close
* Run a full sales process including qualification, demos, proposals, negotiation, and closing
* Keep CRM records updated and organized
* Use AI tools and other sales tools to make outreach and prospecting more efficient
* Work directly with the founders to improve messaging, strategy, and sales process

**Who This Is For**

* Someone with B2B sales, SDR, BDR, account management, or outbound experience
* Someone who wants to move into a more full\-cycle closing role
* Someone comfortable with cold outreach and follow\-up
* Someone who is organized, self\-directed, and motivated by commission
* Someone who wants to get better at sales and close real deals
* EV charging experience is a plus, but not required
* Contractor, construction, or infrastructure\-related experience is also a plus, but not required

**Compensation**

* **$25/hour base** (negotiable)
* **Strong commission structure**
* **Equity offered**
* **Average contract value: $10,000**
* **Fully remote**

**Why This Role Makes Sense**  
This is a good role for someone who wants to earn extra income, get real sales training, and build a track record of closed business. You will not just be setting meetings. You will be working deals from start to finish and getting direct experience in closing.

If you want, I can make this even more **job\-board ready**, like a version that feels right for Indeed or LinkedIn.

Pay: $25\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

Work Location: Remote

title: Nonprofit Virtual Administrative Assistant (NPVA®) | Little Green Light experience wanted
description:
**Nonprofit Virtual Administrative Assistant (NPVA®) \| Little Green Light experience wanted**  
**(1099 Contractor Position)**

The More Than Giving Company is seeking experienced virtual administrative assistants to join our exclusive, top\-of\-the\-line, Nonprofit Virtual Assistant (NPVA®) Service. Join an elite group of contractors working with multiple high\-performing nonprofits.

Our NPVA’s are proactive, organized, and excellent project managers. Our clients look to our NPVA’s for guidance and direction, as well as the ability to independently manage administrative operations.

We are seeking individuals to perform a variety of administrative tasks for nonprofit organizations, with a focus on fundraising specific projects. All activities will be performed virtually.

Applicants with experience utilizing Little Green Light are highly desired!

**NPVA tasks include, but are not limited to:**

* Maintain the organization’s master calendar.
* Manage and update organization’s contact database.
* Schedule and manage board meetings and committee meetings; record and distribute notes; attend monthly board meetings virtually; Assist in board engagement activities.
* Assist in donor engagement and in drafting solicitation letters; Manage and update donor/sponsor information.
* Provide administrative support to fundraising and special events; Maintain event registration.
* Manage email and process general correspondence. Document management and records retention.
* Light bookkeeping; general project management.

**Professional Requirements:**

* Minimum of 2 years as a virtual administrative assistant (no exceptions)
* Minimum of 3 years working with nonprofit organizations (no exceptions)
* Minimum 5 years as an Administrative/Executive Assistant is required (no exceptions)
* College degree is preferred, some college or related certification is required
* Excellent time management, organizational, and written and oral communication skills
* Technical proficiency in MS Office, MailChimp/Constant Contact, Dropbox, Google Suite, Zoom, Trello/Asana, Eventbrite, Calendly, LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram

**Additional:**

* Maintain reliable high\-speed internet connection, computer hardware and software, reliable phone/headset. Hired contractors are required to have their own equipment.
* Be based in the U.S. and agree to a criminal background check.
* Provide 3 references and two writing samples.
* Agree to complete The More than Giving Co NPVA® certification program.
* Secure professional liability insurance.

*The More Than Giving Company is an organization committed to helping nonprofit organizations create sustainable growth. Established in 1998, MTG is a nonprofit fractional staffing company providing all levels of staffing to nonprofit organizations looking to grow and thrive. As a company, we are committed to building and maintaining seasoned nonprofit professional contractors who serve the best interests of our nonprofit clients by delivering best practices, tested solutions, and sustainable advice. To learn more, visit www.morethangiving.co.*

4/20/2026

Job Type: Contract

Pay: $24\.00 per hour

Application Question(s):

* Are you willing to work as a 1099 contractor?
* Are you based in the US?

Experience:

* Little Green Light : 1 year (Required)
* nonprofit: 3 years (Required)
* administrative assistant: 5 years (Required)
* virtual: 1 year (Required)

Work Location: Remote

title: General Counsel (Fractional)
description:
**About Us**



At Sleep Doctor, we're helping millions get a full night's rest. As the most trusted name in sleep health, we make better sleep possible with proven support, clear answers, and hands\-on care that fits real life. Across our brands—SleepDoctor.com, SleepFoundation.org, and SleepApnea.org—we combine clinical expertise with consumer\-first experiences to help people understand their sleep, tackle real challenges, and wake up to healthier, more energized lives. If you're passionate about making better sleep possible—and want to help reshape the future of sleep care—we'd love to meet you.



* \~20\-30 Hours/Week
**About Us**



At Sleep Doctor, we're helping millions get a full night's rest. As the most trusted name in sleep health, we make better sleep possible with proven support, clear answers, and hands\-on care that fits real life.



Across our brands—**SleepDoctor.com**, **SleepFoundation.org**, and **Sleep Apnea.org**—we combine clinical expertise with consumer\-first experiences to help people understand their sleep, tackle real challenges, and wake up to healthier, more energized lives. If you're passionate about making better sleep possible—and want to help reshape the future of sleep care—we'd love to meet you.


**The Opportunity**

We're looking for a seasoned **Fractional General Counsel** to serve as Sleep Doctor's senior legal and compliance leader. This is a hands\-on, high\-ownership role for an attorney who thrives in fast\-moving environments and wants to make a real difference—not just advise from the sidelines.



You'll own our end\-to\-end compliance function, serve as a trusted strategic partner to the executive team, and dramatically reduce our reliance on outside counsel by bringing sharp, practical legal judgment in\-house. This is the right role if you want to build something meaningful at a company that's reshaping the future of sleep health.


**What You'll Own**


**Compliance Program Leadership**


* Design, implement, and continuously improve a comprehensive compliance program spanning DME, telehealth, and digital health operations.
* Serve as the company's Compliance Officer, maintaining all relevant policies, procedures, and training programs across the business.
* Conduct and oversee internal compliance audits; manage any regulatory inquiries or investigations.
* Monitor evolving federal and state regulatory requirements and translate them into operational guidance for the business.

**Healthcare Regulatory**


* Own legal and regulatory matters related to our telehealth platform and DME supply and billing operations, including CMS/Medicare, Medicaid, and state\-level requirements.
* Advise on licensure requirements across all 50 states as we scale, with particular focus on the patchwork of telehealth practice standards and DME supplier licensing that varies significantly by jurisdiction.
* Partner closely with clinical and operations teams to ensure care delivery models remain compliant as the business evolves.

**Data Privacy \& Security**


* Lead HIPAA compliance enterprise\-wide, including Business Associate Agreement management, PHI/PII handling, breach response protocols, and workforce training.
* Oversee compliance with applicable state privacy laws (CCPA, Washington My Health My Data Act, and others).
* Partner with our technology and product teams on privacy\-by\-design practices for our digital health products.

**Commercial \& Employment Law**


* Negotiate and manage key commercial contracts—partner agreements, vendor agreements, strategic partnership structures—with minimal outside counsel involvement.
* Serve as first\-line legal resource for employment law questions, working in close partnership with HR on hiring, separation, policy, and employee relations matters.
* Support the CFO and executive team on governance matters, including board materials and delegation of authority frameworks.

**Strategic Partnership \& Business Development Support**


* Provide legal support on strategic partnerships, including government and federal program relationships (e.g., VA partnerships).
* Advise on deal structuring, term sheet review, and contract negotiation for growth initiatives.

**What We're Looking For**


**Required**


* J.D. from an accredited law school; active bar membership in at least one U.S. jurisdiction.
* 10\+ years of legal experience, with substantial in\-house or law firm experience in healthcare law.
* Deep expertise in DME (Durable Medical Equipment) regulations, including Medicare/Medicaid billing and compliance.
* Hands\-on telehealth regulatory experience across multiple states.
* Demonstrated ownership of a compliance program—you've built or run one, not just advised on one.
* Comprehensive knowledge of HIPAA, PHI/PII handling, and healthcare data privacy.
* Proven ability to function as a resourceful, independent operator in a startup or scaling\-stage business.
* Track record of materially reducing outside counsel spend by handling matters in\-house.

**Strongly Preferred**


* Experience in digital health or health tech—comfortable navigating the intersection of technology, consumer products, and regulated healthcare services.
* Familiarity with FTC regulations applicable to health claims and consumer\-facing digital health content.
* Experience supporting government program partnerships (VA, CMS, etc.).
* Prior experience as a fractional, embedded, or sole in\-house counsel.

**What Success Looks Like**


**In your first 90 days**, you'll have assessed our current compliance posture, prioritized a near\-term remediation roadmap, and established yourself as the go\-to legal voice for the leadership team.


**Within six months**, you'll own end\-to\-end compliance, have meaningfully reduced our outside counsel spend, and be a trusted strategic partner on our most important commercial and regulatory decisions.


**Why Sleep Doctor**


* Mission\-driven company with real clinical impact—we help people sleep better and live healthier lives.
* Lean, high\-trust executive team that values substance over process.
* Opportunity to grow with the organization—this role is built to expand as we do.
* Flexible fractional structure with meaningful ownership and visibility.

**Compensation**


* \~$300\-350/hour \| 20\-30 hours/week
* This is a fractional engagement with potential to grow into a broader role as the company scales.
*Sleep Doctor is committed to bringing together individuals from diverse backgrounds and perspectives. We strive to create an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and be their authentic self, feel a sense of community, and do great work together. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer open to all qualified applicants.*

title: Business Consultant, Exit Advisor (IL)
description:
**Business Consultant \& Exit Advisor (1099\)**
===============================================


### **Exit Factor of Western Chicagoland**


**Help Business Owners Maximize Value. Build Your Own Book of Business.**
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

Exit Factor of Western Chicagoland is seeking experienced professionals who want to turn their business expertise into meaningful consulting work. We help business owners increase profitability, improve operations, and build companies that are more valuable, transferable, and less dependent on the owner.


If you excel in business development, building trust, leading business conversations, and serving as a strategic advisor to business owners, this could be an excellent next chapter of your career.


***Note: Due to the community focus of this role, applicants must live in Chicago's western suburbs to be considered for this role.***


**What Is Exit Factor?**
------------------------


Exit Factor is a business consulting firm. Our passion is helping small to medium\-sized businesses increase their profit, efficiency, and value of their business to prepare for the sale of the company in the future. We do this through business valuations, 1:1 consulting, business partner concierge, education and group programs. We work with companies up to $30 million in revenue across the United States in various industries.


Exit Factor of Chicagoland is a small locally\-owned office that is part of a larger global organization. Therefore, we have the feel of a small business with the resources of a multinational company.


**About the Role**
------------------


This is a relationship\-driven consulting role that blends business development, advisory work, and client management.


You will:


* Build relationships with business owners and referral partners in the local community
* Meet with prospective clients to understand their goals and identify opportunities to help
* Convert qualified prospects into consulting clients
* Lead 1:1 consulting sessions using the Exit Factor framework
* Help clients improve profitability, efficiency, systems, and business value
* Provide accountability, strategic guidance, and access to additional resources
* Partner closely with office owners to deliver a strong client experience


**Who Thrives in This Role**
----------------------------


This is not an entry\-level position. It is best suited for professionals with real business experience and strong executive presence.


Strong candidates often come from backgrounds such as:


* Business owner or exited entrepreneur
* Executive or operator
* Consultant or advisor
* CPA, CFO, or financial services leader
* Sales leader or business development professional
* Self\-funded searchers
* Fractional executive or growth leader


You will do well here if you:


* Build trust quickly and naturally
* Communicate with confidence
* Enjoy strategy and growth conversations
* Are comfortable discussing financials and trade\-offs
* Like networking, prospecting, and creating opportunities
* Work well independently
* Want income tied to performance and results


**Why Join Now**
----------------


This role gives you the chance to build a respected consulting practice while doing work that matters.


The need for exit planning and value\-growth advisory is increasing as more business owners begin thinking about retirement, succession, sale, or long\-term wealth planning. Many owners have significant wealth tied up in their business but are unprepared for transition. Trusted advisors who can help solve that problem are in demand.


As the demand for exit planning grows, so does the Exit Factor brand. Exit Factor is the leading exit planning firm. We are experiencing rapid growth throughout the US and now internationally in the UK, Japan and Australia. Exit Factor was founded by author, thought leader and seasoned business broker Jessica Fialkovich.


**Compensation \& Flexibility**
-------------------------------


* 1099 independent contractor role
* Uncapped, commission\-based compensation
* Revenue share on client accounts you manage
* High income potential for active consultants
* Flexible schedule with part\-time or full\-time potential
* A mix of remote and in\-person work, arranged on your schedule
* Build a book of business that can grow over time


**Training \& Support**
-----------------------


You will not be starting from scratch. We provide:


* Training, onboarding and certification
* Proven consulting frameworks and tools
* Ongoing coaching and continuing education
* Technology systems, marketing and operational support
* Collaboration with local ownership and the Exit Factor network


**This Role May Not Be for You If You Want**
--------------------------------------------


* A fixed salary or hourly wage
* Heavy supervision or rigid structure
* A purely internal role with no client\-facing work
* A position without networking or business development responsibilities
* A beginner\-level role to learn basic business fundamentals


**Ready to Learn More?**
------------------------


If you're ready to use your experience to help business owners build stronger companies, and build something meaningful for yourself, we'd like to hear from you. Apply now!
